summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a Halloween ’85 show with an electrifying Playing in the Band → Ship of Fools transition, standout Mr. Fantasy and a killer Werewolves of London.
review: The recording is a solid 1985 soundboard tape that, despite a few cracks, fade‑outs and a brief drum break, delivers clear vocals and impressive overall fidelity. Musically the show bursts with Halloween energy – the rare Playing in the Band morphing into Ship of Fools, a beautiful jam that lands on Mr. Fantasy, plus superb performances of West L.A. Fadeaway and Werewolves of London.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers gave five stars and praised both the performance (unique transitions, high energy) and the sound quality (clear vocals, high‑quality tape), with only minor technical glitches noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and crisp with strong vocal presence; a few cracks, a sudden fade‑out in "Might as Well", and a brief drum break are noted but considered fixable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Halloween energy fuels a unique Playing in the Band → Ship of Fools transition, followed by an extended melodic jam that resolves into Mr. Fantasy; the set maintains high momentum with strong renditions of West L.A. Fadeaway and Werewolves of London.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Reviewed as improvising around "Hall of the Mountain King" before Funiculi Funicula, showing his playful guitar work.
band_member_comments.Drums: A brief break in the middle of the drums is noted as a recording glitch.
